properties.name: Treaty of Point Elliott Cession Context
properties.nations: Duwamish, Suquamish, Snoqualmie, Snohomish and other Coast Salish signatories
properties.jurisdiction_effect: Shifted large areas of present-day King and Snohomish counties into a U.S. treaty and settlement framework while narrowing where tribal authority could be recognized in later state records.
properties.research_relevance: Useful for reading urban and county MMIP cases against the longer history of land dispossession and fractured legal geography around Puget Sound.
properties.source_note: Illustrative historical context geometry for prototype use only.
properties.notes: Replace with research-validated treaty geography and citations before publication.

properties.name: Treaty of Medicine Creek Cession Context
properties.nations: Nisqually, Puyallup, Squaxin Island and other signatory communities
properties.jurisdiction_effect: Reorganized South Sound territory into settler county and state jurisdictions while later reservation boundaries remained comparatively small and discontinuous.
properties.research_relevance: Helps interpret MMIP cases in Tacoma, Olympia, and adjacent counties where tribal, municipal, and county authority overlap unevenly.
properties.source_note: Illustrative historical context geometry for prototype use only.
properties.notes: Replace with research-validated treaty geography and citations before publication.

properties.name: Allotment and Fee-Patent Fragmentation Context
properties.jurisdiction_effect: Converted many Indigenous land relationships into checkerboarded parcels and competing administrative categories, complicating later policing and court responsibility.
properties.research_relevance: Frames why reservation adjacency does not map cleanly onto the actual jurisdictional conditions surrounding many MMIP cases.
properties.source_note: Illustrative regional context geometry for prototype use only.
properties.notes: This is a conceptual layer, not a parcel-accurate reconstruction.
